goal: Make Archivista AI a credible, maintained, self-hosted Paperless-ngx AI filing project with a public roadmap for coding agents.

positioning.primary_claim: A modern, self-hosted AI filing assistant for Paperless-ngx with strong onboarding, provider choice, owner assignment, custom fields, and automation-ready workflows.
avoid_claims: Do not claim paperless-gpt and paperless-ai are abandoned. As of 2026-06-29, both still show recent GitHub activity.
Do not claim ChatGPT Plus/Pro or Claude Pro/Max browser automation is officially supported until the legal and technical model is validated.
better_claims: Built after paperless-gpt and paperless-ai with current self-hosting workflows in mind.
Focuses on clean setup, modern model routing, owner assignment, custom fields, and homelab-friendly operations.
Explores user-owned subscription workflows as an optional advanced mode where provider terms allow it.

recommended_agent_routing.broad_product_architecture: GLM 5.2 with max reasoning if available; otherwise Codex deep review

phases.name: Trust reset
phases.outcome: The repo no longer looks like a quick fork/rebrand and has a credible OSS surface.

phases.name: Maintainer-ready foundation
phases.outcome: CI, docs, release flow, issue templates, and demo material make contribution and usage feel safe.

phases.name: Differentiating product work
phases.outcome: Archivista AI offers visible advantages over existing Paperless AI tools.

phases.name: Community launch
phases.outcome: Real users, issues, feedback, stars, and release history exist before applying to OSS programs.

tasks.title: Remove stale upstream funding metadata
tasks.context: .github/FUNDING.yml still points to clusterzx accounts, which weakens trust and makes the repo look like an unfinished fork.
acceptance_criteria: .github/FUNDING.yml is removed or replaced with arturict-owned links.
No remaining funding metadata points to another maintainer.
A short PR explains this as project identity cleanup.

tasks.title: Audit legacy names and fork leftovers
tasks.context: Search for paperless-ai, ppai, clusterzx, old icon names, old donation links, and stale docs references.
acceptance_criteria: No user-facing references imply the project is still Paperless-AI unless intentionally used in comparison/history.
Old asset names are either harmless internal filenames or renamed in a focused PR.
README and release notes consistently say Archivista AI.

tasks.title: Add pull-request CI workflow
tasks.context: Current workflows build Docker on release/nightly/manual, but there is no visible PR quality gate.
acceptance_criteria: A workflow runs on pull_request and push to main.
It runs npm ci, npm run typecheck, lint if available, and a minimal smoke test.
The README shows a CI badge only after the workflow is green.

tasks.title: Modernize Docker build workflow
tasks.context: Actions use older major versions and require a GHCR_PAT secret even though GITHUB_TOKEN may be sufficient with package permissions.
acceptance_criteria: actions/checkout, docker/setup-qemu-action, docker/setup-buildx-action, docker/login-action, docker/metadata-action, and docker/build-push-action use current major versions.
GHCR push uses least-privilege permissions where possible.
Nightly image, release semver tags, latest tag, and sha tags are documented.

tasks.title: Replace aggressive stale bot defaults
tasks.context: The stale workflow marks issues stale after 7 days and closes after 3 more. That feels hostile for a small OSS project.
acceptance_criteria: Stale timing is relaxed or workflow is disabled until the project has real issue volume.
Bug reports and feature requests are not closed quickly while adoption is being built.
Maintainer policy is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md.

tasks.title: Fix documented development commands
tasks.context: README says to run npm start, but package.json does not define a start script.
acceptance_criteria: Either package.json includes a working start script or README uses the actual command.
Local development instructions work from a clean clone.
CI validates the documented command path.

tasks.title: Handle missing Paperless thumbnails without crashing
tasks.context: The read-only audit found services that can attempt to write null thumbnail data to disk when Paperless has no thumbnail available.
acceptance_criteria: Missing thumbnail data is handled as a non-fatal condition.
Document analysis can continue with OCR text only when thumbnails are unavailable.
Logs clearly say thumbnail was unavailable without leaking document content.
A regression test covers missing thumbnail responses.

tasks.title: Rewrite README into a launch-quality OSS README
tasks.context: Current README is short and useful but lacks screenshots, architecture, quickstart detail, comparison, support policy, and contribution flow.
acceptance_criteria: README starts with what it does, who it is for, and a screenshot.
Includes Docker Compose quickstart, required Paperless token scopes, provider setup, common failures, and security notes.
Includes a balanced comparison with paperless-gpt and paperless-ai without attacking them.
Includes roadmap, contributing link, license, and release badge.
tasks.agent_fit: kimi-k2.6 for copy, Codex for markdown integration

tasks.title: Create docs pages for provider setup
tasks.context: Provider choice is a major differentiator but needs clear setup docs.
acceptance_criteria: Docs cover OpenRouter, OpenAI API, Ollama, LM Studio/OpenAI-compatible, and Azure.
Each provider page lists required env vars, example values, privacy/cost notes, and troubleshooting.
Docs avoid promising non-API subscription automation until validated.

tasks.title: Add real Fudligagg Lab screenshots
tasks.context: The README and landing page should show a real, anonymized deployment and workflow instead of only abstract screenshots.
acceptance_criteria: Capture setup, dashboard, provider picker, document history, and Paperless before/after metadata from an anonymized real deployment.
All screenshots are anonymized and contain no private documents, names, addresses, tokens, or sensitive metadata.
README and landing page use the screenshots with short captions.

tasks.title: Validate user-owned subscription provider mode
tasks.context: The idea is to let users use existing ChatGPT Plus/Pro, Claude Pro/Max, or opencode-based subscriptions instead of paying for API keys. This needs legal, UX, and reliability validation before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Document what is allowed by OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, and opencode terms for automated access.
Define a safe architecture that does not ask users to share account credentials with Archivista AI.
If viable, design a local adapter interface where the user runs their own agent bridge locally.
If not viable, document why API/local providers remain the supported path.
tasks.agent_fit: GLM 5.2 max reasoning or Codex deep research

tasks.title: Design provider adapter interface
tasks.context: Archivista should support API providers cleanly now and optional local agent bridges later.
acceptance_criteria: There is a ProviderAdapter interface with analyzeDocument capabilities, model metadata, healthcheck, and privacy/cost metadata.
OpenAI/OpenRouter/Ollama/Compatible implementations fit the same shape.
Future opencode/Claude Code/local browser bridge adapters can be added without touching document-processing logic.

tasks.title: Add dry-run review mode
tasks.context: Users need to trust AI metadata before allowing writes to Paperless.
acceptance_criteria: A setting allows AI suggestions without writing metadata.
Dashboard shows proposed title, tags, correspondent, document type, custom fields, and owner.
User can approve, reject, or apply selected fields.
Audit history stores model, prompt version, and applied changes.

tasks.title: Add confidence scoring and guardrails
tasks.context: Owner assignment and filing fields should avoid bad guesses.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt asks model for confidence per field.
Configurable thresholds decide auto-apply vs review-required.
Low-confidence owner/correspondent changes are never auto-applied by default.
Dashboard explains why a suggestion was held for review.

tasks.title: Build Paperless metadata diff engine
tasks.context: Users should see exactly what Archivista changed in Paperless.
acceptance_criteria: Before/after values are captured for each modified field.
History page can filter by field, document, model, and apply status.
Failed writes are visible with error messages and retry options.
Sensitive values are not leaked into public logs.

tasks.title: Improve custom fields support
tasks.context: Custom fields are a key Paperless power-user feature and a source of API edge cases.
acceptance_criteria: Field types are discovered from Paperless and shown in settings.
The model receives field labels, types, allowed values, and examples.
Invalid model outputs are rejected before PATCH/PUT.
A fixture test covers text, date, boolean, monetary, select, and URL-like fields if Paperless supports them.

tasks.title: Add multilingual OCR normalization strategy
tasks.context: German/French documents can suffer from OCR quirks around umlauts and accents. Archivista can help by normalizing text before matching/classification.
acceptance_criteria: Preprocessing keeps original OCR text but also builds a normalized matching copy.
German umlauts, French accents, currency formats, and Swiss date/address formats are tested.
Prompt tells the model to preserve original spelling in output where appropriate.

tasks.title: Replace illustrative hero card with real product screenshot
tasks.context: The landing page is polished but too abstract for a utility OSS project. Real UI screenshots will convert better and feel more credible.
acceptance_criteria: Hero uses a real anonymized Archivista dashboard/setup screenshot.
The first viewport shows what the app actually looks like.
Mobile layout keeps text and screenshot readable without overlap.

tasks.title: Add install and demo sections to landing page
tasks.context: The current site is mostly value-prop copy. OSS users need proof and a quick path to try it.
acceptance_criteria: Add Docker Compose quickstart section.
Add screenshot-driven workflow: connect Paperless, choose provider, review result, write back metadata.
Add GitHub release and docs links.
Add clear note that the public website does not process documents.
tasks.agent_fit: kimi-k2.6 for copy, Codex for implementation

tasks.title: Create first public issue set
tasks.context: A serious OSS repo should show planned work publicly instead of hiding everything in private notes.
acceptance_criteria: Create 10-15 GitHub issues from this roadmap.
Label issues as good first issue, documentation, provider, paperless-integration, ci, security, or roadmap.
Use issue text that sounds human, specific, and non-generic.
Do not spam; each issue should map to a real PR-sized change.
tasks.agent_fit: kimi-k2.6 for issue text, Codex for gh issue create

tasks.title: Write launch post for r/selfhosted and Paperless communities
tasks.context: The launch should ask for testers, not stars. It should be transparent about early status and AI assistance if required.
acceptance_criteria: Post explains the real problem: scanned documents still need cleanup after OCR.
Includes screenshots, Docker Compose, GitHub link, and clear early-stage caveats.
Avoids trashing paperless-gpt or paperless-ai.
Mentions AI-assisted writing only where community rules require it.

tasks.title: Investigate Paperless workflow timing issue #12117
tasks.context: Paperless issue #12117 says Document Added workflows may run before OCR text and metadata matching are ready. This overlaps with Archivista's automation needs.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduce on a local Paperless-ngx dev setup.
Identify whether docs, workflow trigger order, or implementation is wrong.
Submit either a small docs PR or a focused code PR upstream.
Reference how reliable post-consume metadata hooks help external automation tools.

tasks.title: Investigate Paperless custom field API edge cases
tasks.context: Several historical Paperless issues mention custom field update surprises. Archivista depends on reliable metadata writes.
acceptance_criteria: Check current Paperless behavior against latest main, not old reports only.
Write a small reproducible test or docs clarification if behavior is confusing.
Only open an upstream PR if the issue still exists or docs are objectively unclear.

tasks.title: Investigate OCR normalization docs for DACH/French documents
tasks.context: Older issues mention umlaut/accent OCR problems. Even if not a Paperless bug, docs or troubleshooting improvements could help users and tie into Archivista's target audience.
acceptance_criteria: Verify whether current Paperless docs already cover OCR language packs and PDF text extraction behavior.
If a gap exists, propose a docs PR with a DACH/French troubleshooting section.
Keep the PR helpful to Paperless itself, not promotional for Archivista.
